UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores additional user-specific information, linked to Firebase Authentication. Each user's unique identifier from Firebase Auth (UID) serves as the primary key for this entity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity, typically matching the Firebase Authentication user ID (UID).

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for display and communication purposes.

displayName.description: The user's chosen display name, used for personalization within the app.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

DiagnosticResult.title: DiagnosticResult
DiagnosticResult.description: Represents a single instance of a medical image upload, its processing status, and the corresponding AI diagnostic prediction. This entity captures the complete lifecycle of a diagnostic request.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiagnosticResult entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity that initiated this diagnostic request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DiagnosticResult)

imageStoragePath.description: The full path or URL to the uploaded medical image in Firebase Storage.

originalFileName.description: The original filename of the medical image uploaded by the user.

uploadTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the medical image was initially uploaded to Firebase Storage and the diagnostic process was initiated.

diagnosisTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the AI model completed the diagnosis and the result was recorded. This field is populated when 'status' is 'COMPLETED'.

diseasePrediction.description: The primary disease predicted by the custom PyTorch CNN model (e.g., 'Pneumonia', 'Normal', 'COVID-19'). This field is populated when 'status' is 'COMPLETED'.

confidenceScore.description: The confidence score (ranging from 0.0 to 1.0) associated with the AI's disease prediction. This field is populated when 'status' is 'COMPLETED'.

modelVersion.description: Identifier for the specific version of the PyTorch CNN model used for this diagnosis, ensuring traceability and reproducibility of results. This field is populated when 'status' is 'COMPLETED'.

status.description: The current status of the diagnostic process (e.g., 'PENDING', 'PROCESSING', 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED').

errorReason.description: Optional field to store the reason or error message if the diagnostic process failed. This field is populated when 'status' is 'FAILED'.

definition.description: Stores user-specific profile information. Each document's ID corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID, ensuring direct ownership. Access is strictly limited to the authenticated user matching the 'userId' parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores individual diagnostic results, including image processing status and AI predictions, for a specific user. Each document includes a denormalized 'userId' field to explicitly link to the owner for authorization independence. Access is strictly limited to the authenticated user matching the 'userId' path parameter.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this diagnostic result, matching their Firebase Authentication UID and the parent UserProfile.

params.name: diagnosticResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific diagnostic result document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure robust security and simplified rule management.Two main top-level collections are established: one for user profiles and one for the diagnostic results, organized hierarchically.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding the `userId` directly into every `DiagnosticResult` document. While `DiagnosticResult` documents are nested under a user's profile (`/user_profiles/{userId}/diagnostic_results/{diagnosticResultId}`), the presence of `diagnosticResult.userId` allows security rules to evaluate ownership without requiring a `get()` operation on the parent `UserProfile` document. This means atomic writes for creating both a user profile and their first diagnostic result can be secured independently, adhering to the critical principle.

**QAPs (Queries are not Filters):** The structure inherently supports secure `list` operations. By nesting `DiagnosticResult` documents under `/user_profiles/{userId}/diagnostic_results`, security rules can enforce that `request.auth.uid` must match the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This ensures that users can only query and retrieve their own diagnostic results. There's no mixing of data with different security postures, as all documents within a user's `diagnostic_results` subcollection belong exclusively to that user. Similarly, the `/user_profiles/{userId}` collection strictly contains documents owned by the corresponding user, preventing cross-user data access during list operations.
